prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "弹力带踝外翻" (banded ankle eversion) exercise, front view, focused on lower leg and foot. Person posture: Athletic person seated on the floor with the right leg extended STRAIGHT FORWARD, the right HEEL resting on the floor and the toes pointing slightly upward. The left leg is bent with the foot tucked back behind the buttocks, completely OUT OF THE WAY. A blue elastic loop resistance band is wrapped in a U-SHAPE around the FOREFOOT of the right foot (around the ball / metatarsals area). Both ENDS of the U-shaped band exit the foot on the INNER (BIG-TOE / MEDIAL) side of the foot — they run together as a parallel pair from the medial side of the right forefoot, downward and to the LEFT, ending at a small fixed floor anchor on the LEFT side of the body (medial to the right foot). The two band strands form a clearly visible TUG of resistance pulling the forefoot toward the LEFT/MEDIAL side. The right ankle is shown ROTATED OUTWARD against this medial pull (ankle eversion): the SOLE of the right foot tilts so it visibly faces the FAR RIGHT, away from the body's midline. The BIG-TOE edge of the foot is HIGH, the PINKY-TOE edge is LOW. View: front-three-quarter so both the band wrap on the forefoot AND the foot rotation are clearly visible. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the resistance band must clearly WRAP AROUND the OUTSIDE of the forefoot like a strap around a package — it must NOT emerge from inside the foot, must NOT pass through any body part, and the foot must be solidly visible underneath the wrap.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: peroneal muscles (peroneus longus and brevis, outer side of the lower leg). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: tibialis anterior and small intrinsic foot muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腓骨长短肌" pointing to the peroneal muscles (outer lower leg); "胫骨前肌" pointing to the tibialis anterior (assisting); "足部稳定肌" pointing to the foot stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
